response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js track record, but he does not meet the mandatory requirements for this role in any material way. His C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE and a single academic thesis project, falling far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, or Capital Markets domain knowledge present; his closest financial exposure is RIDE Capital, a retail investment platform built on Node.js/TypeScript/GraphQL. SQL Server and Oracle are not mentioned anywhere, and Windows-platform development is entirely absent from his history.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) consistently applied across multiple senior roles
Strong communication skills evidenced by conference talks (FOSS4G), public workshops, and active open-source project leadership
Some financial-services domain adjacency through RIDE Capital (investment platform) and general experience with high-throughput, low-latency systems at Dunnhumby
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus one academic project) versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
Zero Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, or Capital Markets domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience; Windows-platform development is entirely absent from a career spent primarily on Linux/cloud stacks

key_factors.factor: No capital markets or fixed income domain
